---
title: "Figure 5 — where MacroIR itself fails, and which part of the distortion does it"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
THE PAPER'S HONEST FIGURE. Figures 1-4 show IR calibrated everywhere they look; an approximation that
never fails would mean the validation machinery is a demonstration, not a test. This figure locates
IR's own failure and attributes it.

PLANE: one panel per N_ch (columns), x = Delta*k_off (the 7 interval values swept inside every cell,
so the x resolution is free), y = instrumental noise (6 levels for IR). macro_IR only: MR and VR have
done their work by Figure 4 and are not carried further.

BLOCKS:
  A  TOTAL distortion, one row per parameter, ALL FIVE. Not a selection: showing every direction is
     what makes the cancellation claim verifiable (k_on and k_off inflate, N_ch deflates, i and
     sigma_noise stay null), and the two null rows are themselves the result that the failure is
     confined to the kinetic and count directions. Measured over the 24 cells x 7 intervals, the
     fraction of points departing by >=15% is k_off 15%, N_ch 6%, k_on 3.6%, i 0%, sigma_noise 0%.
  B  DECOMPOSITION into sample and correlation, on k[off] only, because that is the direction that
     carries the failure. The choice is stated in the caption rather than left implicit, and the
     caption also reports the N_ch attribution (corr 0.68 vs sample 0.94 at the corner) so the reader
     is not left to assume it follows k_off.

Row identity lives in a right-hand facet STRIP, not in the y-axis title: a row label is not an axis
label, and at this size a parenthetical inside the axis title is unreadable.

Reading: colour is the CI-shrunk distortion, so a cell whose 95% bootstrap interval brackets 1 is
drawn exactly white. Every departure with colour is significant at 95%, and the value shown is the
conservative bound, not the point estimate. Ratio > 1 means the score varies more than the Fisher
claims, i.e. the reported interval is too NARROW (over-confident); < 1 means too wide.

ANCHOR: battery_pool_G, i.e. the distortion at the optimum theta_pool, which is the anchor the plan
settles on for distortion. battery_sim_G agrees to < 0.005 for N_ch >= 50 and is slightly larger at
N_ch = 10, so the choice does not drive the result.

NOT PLOTTED, on purpose: the log-determinant decomposition. log det GIDM = log det GSDM + log det CDM
is EXACT (verified to 1e-14), but a log-determinant is a volume and cancels an inflation in one
parameter direction against a deflation in another - exactly the cancellation row B exists to expose.
The additivity belongs in the caption as the exact statement; the map stays per-parameter and signed.
Do NOT use Likelihood_Information_Distortion_Reconstituted: it implements an identity that is false
(the exact relation is a congruence, GIDM = K.CDM.K', not SDM^1/2.CDM.SDM^1/2) and is NaN here.

TRAP: the "noise" label is not the physical noise. current_noise = label/1000, and the CSV column
noise_in_conductance_tau stores the LABEL. The y axis below is the label, and the caption says so.

Supersedes figure_5_master{,_affine,_frobenius}.Rmd, moved to ../archive/paper_superseded_20260722/.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse); library(patchwork)
```

```{r params}
ALGO   <- "macro_IR"
# 10 / 50 / 200 / 1000 rather than the decade grid: the transition from failure to calibration happens
# between 10 and 1000, and a 10000 column renders almost entirely white. Chosen to sample the change.
NCHS   <- c(10, 20, 50, 100, 1000, 10000)               # six columns: the transition, then the clean end
NOISES <- c("0.05", "0.1", "0.2", "0.5", "1", "10")     # labels; physical noise = label/1000
DATA   <- "../data/1c2ae6f"

PIDX      <- c(on = 0, off = 1, unitary_current = 2, Current_Noise = 3, Num_ch_mean = 5)
PARAM_LAB <- c(on = "log[10]~k[on]", off = "log[10]~k[off]", unitary_current = "log[10]~i",
               Current_Noise = "log[10]~sigma[noise]", Num_ch_mean = "log[10]~N[ch]")
COMPS <- c(total  = "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Gaussian_Information_Distortion",
           sample = "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Sample_Distortion",
           corr   = "Probit_statistics_Likelihood_Correlation_Distortion")

RAMP <- c("#021024","#053061","#2166AC","#4393C3","#92C5DE","#D1E5F0","#F7F7F7",
          "#FDDBC7","#F4A582","#D6604D","#B2182B","#67001F","#3d0011")
bandcols <- function(breaks) {
  n <- length(breaks) - 1; ctr <- findInterval(0, breaks); nb <- ctr - 1; nr <- n - ctr; K <- max(nb, nr, 1)
  blue <- colorRampPalette(rev(RAMP[1:7]), space = "Lab")(K + 1)
  red  <- colorRampPalette(RAMP[7:13],     space = "Lab")(K + 1)
  cols <- rep(RAMP[7], n)
  if (nb > 0) cols[seq(ctr - 1, 1)] <- blue[2:(nb + 1)]
  if (nr > 0) cols[seq(ctr + 1, n)] <- red[2:(nr + 1)]
  cols
}
fmt_big <- function(x) formatC(x, format = "g", digits = 3)
DIST_LIN    <- c(0.5, 0.67, 0.8, 0.87, 0.91, 0.94, 0.97, 0.99, 1.01, 1.03, 1.06, 1.1, 1.15, 1.25, 1.5, 1.7, 2.5)
DIST_BREAKS <- log10(DIST_LIN)
DIST_PAL    <- bandcols(DIST_BREAKS)
DIST_LAB    <- fmt_big(DIST_LIN)

# bias is in dex and signed, so its own symmetric scale and its own key
# range set from the data, not by habit: over the 24 cells the CI-shrunk bias spans -0.004 to
# +0.064 dex and is significant in 17% of points, so a +-0.2 scale would compress everything that
# happens into three bands
BIAS_LIN    <- c(-0.02, -0.01, -0.005, -0.002, 0.002, 0.005, 0.01, 0.02, 0.03, 0.05, 0.07)
BIAS_PAL    <- bandcols(BIAS_LIN)
BIAS_LAB    <- formatC(BIAS_LIN, format = "g", digits = 2)
```

```{r data}
# every (N_ch, noise) cell must be present: a missing one would silently shorten the noise axis
paths <- expand_grid(nch = NCHS, z = NOISES) %>%
  mutate(f = sprintf("%s/figure_3_G_nch_%d_nsim_10000_%s_noise_%s_battery_pool_G.csv", DATA, nch, ALGO, z))
missing <- paths$f[!file.exists(paths$f)]
if (length(missing)) stop("figure_5: missing cells:\n  ", paste(basename(missing), collapse = "\n  "))
cat("figure_5:", nrow(paths), "cells,", ALGO, "at N_ch", paste(NCHS, collapse = "/"),
    "x noise", paste(NOISES, collapse = "/"), "\n")

# BIAS comes from battery_sim_G, anchored at theta_sim. It cannot come from the pool files: a bias
# evaluated at the optimum is zero by construction because the score vanishes there.
bias <- pmap_dfr(paths, function(nch, z, f)
    read.csv(sub("_battery_pool_G", "_battery_sim_G", f), skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
      mutate(noise = z)) %>%
  filter(component_path == "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Distortion_Induced_Bias",
         param_index %in% PIDX[c("off", "Num_ch_mean")],
         statistic == "value", probit %in% c("mean", "quantile")) %>%
  mutate(key = case_when(probit == "mean" ~ "m", quantile_level == 0.025 ~ "lo",
                         quantile_level == 0.975 ~ "hi", TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(key)) %>%
  group_by(noise, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_index, key) %>%
  summarise(v = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = key, values_from = v) %>%
  # CI-aware toward ZERO: a cell whose interval covers 0 renders white
  mutate(Bconf = ifelse(m > 0, pmax(0, lo), pmin(0, hi)),
         lx = log10(interval_in_tau), ly = log10(as.numeric(noise)),
         param = names(PIDX)[match(param_index, PIDX)],
         nchf  = factor(paste0("N[ch]==", Num_ch), levels = paste0("N[ch]==", NCHS)))

stat <- pmap_dfr(paths, function(nch, z, f)
    read.csv(f, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>% mutate(noise = z)) %>%
  filter(component_path %in% COMPS, param_index == param_col, param_index %in% PIDX,
         statistic == "value", probit %in% c("mean", "quantile")) %>%
  mutate(comp = names(COMPS)[match(component_path, COMPS)],
         key  = case_when(probit == "mean" ~ "m", quantile_level == 0.025 ~ "lo",
                          quantile_level == 0.975 ~ "hi", TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(key)) %>%
  group_by(comp, noise, Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_index, key) %>%
  summarise(v = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = key, values_from = v) %>%
  # CI-aware: a cell whose 95% interval brackets 1 collapses to exactly 1 and renders white
  mutate(Dconf = ifelse(m > 1, pmax(1, lo), pmin(1, hi)),
         lx = log10(interval_in_tau), ly = log10(as.numeric(noise)),
         z  = pmin(pmax(log10(Dconf), min(DIST_BREAKS)), max(DIST_BREAKS)),
         param = names(PIDX)[match(param_index, PIDX)],
         nchf  = factor(paste0("N[ch]==", Num_ch), levels = paste0("N[ch]==", NCHS)))
```



```{r points}
# The two illustrative design points. Panel A shows what the algorithm does at each; every map panel
# below carries them as numbered markers, so the abstract surface and the concrete ellipse are the
# same fact seen twice. Point 2 was chosen from the data, not by eye: all five parameter directions
# sit at exactly 1 there, with no departure significant at 95%.
# THREE points, and the middle one is a control, not decoration. At the failing point two things act
# at once: the algorithm's distortion and the estimator's own finite-sample departure from normality,
# which at N_ch = 10 does not vanish even at group_size 100. A distant calibrated cell does not
# separate them, because it also changes N_ch and with it that finite-sample term. Point 2 holds
# group size, N_ch and noise FIXED and moves only the interval, so the difference between 1 and 2 is
# the algorithm alone. Point 3 is a design point calibrated in every direction, for scale.
ELL_POINTS <- tibble::tribble(
  ~lab, ~nch,   ~noise,  ~dlt,  ~what,
  "1",  20,     "0.05",  0.01,  "distorted",
  "2",  20,     "0.05",  1,     "biased",
  "3",  10000,  "0.5",   0.1,   "faithful")
# N_ch = 10 AND group_size = 100, and both halves were tested rather than assumed. Measured at the
# corner (Delta 0.01, noise 0.05), ellipse ratios against what the map reports there (1.62 and 0.65):
#   N_ch  10, group  10 (1000 fits):  k_off 1.31, N_ch 1.58  <- SIGN FLIPPED on N_ch
#   N_ch  10, group 100 ( 100 fits):  k_off 1.34, N_ch 0.83  <- both signs right
#   N_ch  50, group 100 ( 100 fits):  k_off 1.06, N_ch 0.93  <- failure nearly invisible
#   N_ch 200, group  10 (1000 fits):  k_off 0.93, N_ch 1.01  <- reads CALIBRATED, contradicts the map
# More fits would be welcome (a variance from 100 fits carries 14% relative uncertainty, against 4.5%
# from 1000), but group 10 at few channels does not merely add noise: its finite-sample inflation
# reverses the sign of the N_ch direction, which is the one thing this panel exists to show. And at
# larger N_ch the ellipse stops seeing the failure at all, because it compares Cov(theta_hat) with
# G^-1, i.e. the sandwich MARGINALISED over the other parameters, which dilutes what the map's
# unmarginalised J-versus-G comparison sees. The 14% is the price of a panel that agrees with its map.
ELL_GROUP <- 100
```

```{r ellipses, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 2.5}
# GROUP SIZE 100, NOT 10. Each MLE is fitted to `group_size` recordings, so at 10 the estimator carries
# a finite-sample variance inflation that belongs to the group size, not to the algorithm: on point 1's
# cell the empirical/Fisher ELLIPSE AREA ratio is 2.22 (group 10) against 1.15 (group 100) at
# Delta*k_off = 1, 1.33 vs 0.90 at 0.5 and 1.55 vs 1.21 at 0.01. Excess kurtosis is small at both
# (-0.2 to 0.8), so it is the O(1/n) term, not heavy tails. The MAPS below fit no estimates at all and
# are untouched by this.
PAIR  <- c(off = "off", nch = "Num_ch_mean")     # the two opposite-signed directions
win   <- function(v, k = 4) { m <- median(v, na.rm = TRUE); c(m - k * mad(v, na.rm = TRUE), m + k * mad(v, na.rm = TRUE)) }
ell   <- function(S, cx, cy, n = 200) {          # 95% ellipse, 2 dof
  if (any(!is.finite(S))) return(NULL)
  e <- eigen(S, symmetric = TRUE); if (any(e$values <= 0)) return(NULL)
  th <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n)
  P <- e$vectors %*% diag(sqrt(e$values * qchisq(0.95, 2))) %*% rbind(cos(th), sin(th))
  tibble(x = cx + P[1, ], y = cy + P[2, ])
}

panel <- function(i) {
  pt <- ELL_POINTS[i, ]
  stem <- sprintf("%s/figure_3_G_nch_%d_nsim_10000_%s_noise_%s", DATA, pt$nch, ALGO, pt$noise)
  pts <- read.csv(paste0(stem, "_mle_cloud_runs.csv"), skip = 1) %>%
    filter(component_path == "Model_Parameters_Hat", statistic == "value",
           group_size == ELL_GROUP, param_name %in% PAIR,
           abs(interval_in_tau - pt$dlt) < 1e-9) %>%
    select(sample_index, param_name, value) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = param_name, values_from = value)
  bat <- read.csv(paste0(stem, "_battery_pool_G.csv"), skip = 1) %>%
    filter(statistic == "value", probit == "mean", abs(interval_in_tau - pt$dlt) < 1e-9,
           param_index %in% PIDX[PAIR], param_col %in% PIDX[PAIR])
  cov2 <- function(cp) {
    d <- bat %>% filter(component_path == cp); m <- matrix(NA_real_, 2, 2); id <- unname(PIDX[PAIR])
    for (a in 1:2) for (b in 1:2) { v <- d$value[d$param_index == id[a] & d$param_col == id[b]]
                                    if (length(v) == 1) m[a, b] <- v }
    m / ELL_GROUP
  }
  cx <- mean(pts$off, na.rm = TRUE); cy <- mean(pts$Num_ch_mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  E  <- cov(cbind(pts$off, pts$Num_ch_mean), use = "complete.obs")
  # TWO ellipses, not three. The panel asks one question - does the reported uncertainty match the
  # real one - and the sandwich-corrected ellipse answers a different one that Figure 2 already
  # answers for four algorithms. It coincides with the empirical at two of these three points, so it
  # would add ink without adding a claim; its ratio is reported in the caption as a number instead.
  src <- bind_rows(
    mutate(ell(E, cx, cy), source = "empirical"),
    mutate(ell(cov2("Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Fisher_Covariance"), cx, cy), source = "Fisher"),
    mutate(ell(cov2("Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Distortion_Corrected_Covariance"), cx, cy),
           source = "corrected")) %>%
    mutate(source = factor(source, levels = c("empirical", "Fisher", "corrected")))
  A <- function(S) { e <- eigen(S, symmetric = TRUE)$values
                     if (any(!is.finite(S)) || any(e <= 0)) NA_real_ else sqrt(prod(e)) }
  rF <- A(E) / A(cov2("Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Fisher_Covariance"))
  rC <- A(E) / A(cov2("Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Distortion_Corrected_Covariance"))
  # PER-AXIS ratios, one per parameter, rather than one area. An area multiplies an inflated
  # direction by a deflated one and hides both, which is the very cancellation the maps below exist
  # to expose; a single number on this panel would invite the reading the rest of the figure refutes.
  Fm <- cov2("Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Fisher_Covariance")
  rx <- E[1, 1] / Fm[1, 1]      # k_off, the x axis
  ry <- E[2, 2] / Fm[2, 2]      # N_ch, the y axis
  # A variance estimated from n fits carries a chi-square uncertainty of its own (about 14% relative
  # at n = 100). The multiplier is therefore COLOURED by the same CI-shrunk rule and the same ramp the
  # maps use: a ratio whose interval covers 1 renders in the neutral band, so a number like 0.91 is
  # visibly "not different from 1" rather than an unexplained departure.
  nf   <- nrow(pts)
  shrink <- function(r) { lo <- r * (nf - 1) / qchisq(.975, nf - 1)
                          hi <- r * (nf - 1) / qchisq(.025, nf - 1)
                          if (r > 1) max(1, lo) else min(1, hi) }
  # colour ties the number to the map: the same ramp, on the CI-shrunk value. But a ratio that is not
  # distinguishable from 1 lands in the neutral band, which is near-white and therefore invisible on a
  # white panel - so that case is drawn in grey instead, which also says "not significant" honestly.
  bandcol <- function(r) { sr <- shrink(r)
    if (isTRUE(all.equal(sr, 1))) "grey45"
    else DIST_PAL[max(1, min(length(DIST_PAL),
           findInterval(log10(sr), DIST_BREAKS, rightmost.closed = TRUE)))] }

  # Figure 2's marker grammar: cross = truth, filled circle = empirical mean, open circle = truth
  # plus the bias the distortion theory PREDICTS (distortion-induced bias, anchored at theta_sim, so
  # it is a prediction and not a re-description of the cloud).
  dib <- read.csv(paste0(stem, "_battery_sim_G.csv"), skip = 1) %>%
    filter(component_path == "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Distortion_Induced_Bias",
           statistic == "value", probit == "mean", abs(interval_in_tau - pt$dlt) < 1e-9,
           param_index %in% PIDX[PAIR]) %>%
    select(param_index, value)
  gx <- 2; gy <- log10(pt$nch)
  # PAIR maps the short key to the parameter name; PIDX maps the parameter name to its index
  gv <- function(nm) { v <- dib$value[dib$param_index == PIDX[[PAIR[[nm]]]]]
                       if (length(v) == 1) v else NA_real_ }
  mk <- tibble(x = c(gx, cx, gx + gv("off")), y = c(gy, cy, gy + gv("nch")),
               kind = factor(c("truth", "empirical mean", "predicted (truth + bias)"),
                             levels = c("truth", "empirical mean", "predicted (truth + bias)")))

  # symmetric window CENTRED ON THE TRUTH, so a displacement reads as a displacement and the three
  # panels share one visual grammar despite spanning very different scales
  # symmetric about the truth on both axes; the y half-width carries extra headroom so the two
  # multipliers sit on white space rather than on the ellipse, without breaking the symmetry
  hx <- 1.10 * max(abs(c(src$x, pts$off, mk$x) - gx), na.rm = TRUE)
  hy <- 1.34 * max(abs(c(src$y, pts$Num_ch_mean, mk$y) - gy), na.rm = TRUE)

  cat(sprintf(paste0("figure_5A point %s (%s): N_ch %d, noise %s, Delta %.2f, n_fits %d | ",
                     "per-axis emp/Fisher  k_off %.2f  N_ch %.2f | area emp/Fisher %.2f, ",
                     "emp/corrected %.2f\n"),
              pt$lab, pt$what, pt$nch, pt$noise, pt$dlt, nrow(pts), rx, ry, rF, rC))
  ggplot() +
    geom_point(data = pts, aes(off, Num_ch_mean), colour = "grey70", size = .2, alpha = .4) +
    geom_path(data = src, aes(x, y, colour = source, linetype = source, linewidth = source)) +
    geom_point(data = mk, aes(x, y, shape = kind), size = 1.7, stroke = .55, fill = "white") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c(truth = 3, `empirical mean` = 16, `predicted (truth + bias)` = 21)) +
    # the ratios are of VARIANCES, matching the map's colour scale and Figure 2's area ratio. The eye
    # measures the ellipse in standard deviations, so the visible width ratio is their square root:
    # said once here rather than left for the reader to guess.
    # on a white plate, so the number stays legible over the cloud and the ellipses
    annotate("label", x = gx + hx, y = gy + hy, hjust = 1.03, vjust = 1.15, size = 2.2,
             colour = bandcol(rx), fill = "white", label.size = 0,
             label.padding = unit(0.6, "pt"), parse = TRUE,
             label = sprintf("k[off]*\" %.2f\u00d7\"", rx)) +
    annotate("label", x = gx + hx, y = gy + hy, hjust = 1.03, vjust = 2.5, size = 2.2,
             colour = bandcol(ry), fill = "white", label.size = 0,
             label.padding = unit(0.6, "pt"), parse = TRUE,
             label = sprintf("N[ch]*\" %.2f\u00d7\"", ry)) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(empirical = "grey35", Fisher = "#D55E00", corrected = "#0072B2")) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = c(empirical = "solid", Fisher = "dashed", corrected = "dotted")) +
    scale_linewidth_manual(values = c(empirical = .5, Fisher = .7, corrected = .85), guide = "none") +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(gx - hx, gx + hx), ylim = c(gy - hy, gy + hy)) +
    labs(x = expression(log[10]~k[off]), y = expression(log[10]~N[ch]),
         title = paste0(pt$lab, "   ", pt$what),
         subtitle = bquote(N[ch] == .(pt$nch) ~ "," ~ sigma == .(pt$noise) ~ "," ~
                           Delta %.% k[off] == .(pt$dlt))) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 8, base_family = "Helvetica") +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          plot.title = element_text(size = 7.5, hjust = .5, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 1)),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 6, hjust = .5, margin = margin(b = 2)),
          axis.text = element_text(size = 6), axis.title = element_text(size = 7.5),
          aspect.ratio = 1,
          legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank(), legend.box = "horizontal",
          legend.text = element_text(size = 6.5), legend.key.size = unit(.6, "lines"),
          legend.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0), legend.box.spacing = unit(2, "pt"),
          plot.margin = margin(2, 4, 2, 4))
}
gA <- wrap_plots(lapply(seq_len(nrow(ELL_POINTS)), panel), nrow = 1, guides = "collect") &
        theme(legend.position = "bottom")
gA
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7, fig.height = 8.2}
X_BRK <- log10(c(.01, .1, 1)); Y_BRK <- log10(c(.05, .5, 10))
# the roster of the maps is exactly the pair the ellipses show, so panel A and the maps are the same
# two parameters seen twice. The other three directions are reported in the text, not drawn.
PARAM_ORD   <- c("off", "Num_ch_mean")
PARAM_STRIP <- c(off = "log[10]~k[off]", Num_ch_mean = "log[10]~N[ch]")

marks <- function(rows) ELL_POINTS %>%
  mutate(lx = log10(dlt), ly = log10(as.numeric(noise)),
         nchf = factor(paste0("N[ch]==", nch), levels = paste0("N[ch]==", NCHS))) %>%
  tidyr::crossing(prow = factor(rows, levels = rows))

mapblock <- function(d, zvar, pal, brk, top = FALSE, bottom = FALSE) {
  if ("comp" %in% names(d)) d <- filter(d, comp == "total")   # the maps show the total, not its parts
  d <- d %>% filter(param %in% PARAM_ORD) %>%
    mutate(prow = factor(PARAM_STRIP[param], levels = unname(PARAM_STRIP[PARAM_ORD])),
           zz = pmin(pmax(zvar(.data), min(brk)), max(brk)))
  mk <- marks(levels(d$prow))
  ggplot(d, aes(lx, ly, z = zz)) +
    geom_contour_filled(breaks = brk) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal, drop = FALSE, guide = "none") +
    geom_point(size = .08, colour = "grey35", alpha = .35) +
    geom_point(data = mk, aes(lx, ly), inherit.aes = FALSE, shape = 21, fill = "white",
               colour = "black", size = 1.5, stroke = .45) +
    geom_text(data = mk, aes(lx, ly, label = lab), inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 1.9,
              fontface = "bold", vjust = -0.95) +
    facet_grid(prow ~ nchf, labeller = label_parsed) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = X_BRK, labels = c(".01", ".1", "1")) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = Y_BRK, labels = c(".05", ".5", "10")) +
    labs(y = "noise") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 8, base_family = "Helvetica") +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.spacing = unit(0.12, "cm"),
          legend.position = "none", axis.text = element_text(size = 6),
          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8), strip.text.y = element_text(size = 7, angle = 0),
          plot.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)) +
    (if (top) theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 7.5)) else theme(strip.text.x = element_blank())) +
    (if (bottom) labs(x = expression(Delta %.% k[off])) else
       theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank()))
}

colorbar <- function(pal, edge_lab, title, lin, sol = numeric(0), dsh = numeric(0)) {
  n <- length(pal); pos <- function(v) match(v, lin) - 0.5
  mkx <- c(
    if (length(dsh)) list(annotate("segment", x = pos(dsh), xend = pos(dsh), y = 0.5, yend = 2.0,
                                   colour = "grey25", linewidth = .35, linetype = "dashed")),
    if (length(sol)) list(annotate("segment", x = pos(sol), xend = pos(sol), y = 0.5, yend = 2.0,
                                   colour = "black", linewidth = .5)))
  ggplot(data.frame(x = seq_len(n)), aes(x, 1, fill = factor(x))) +
    geom_tile(colour = "grey92", linewidth = .15) + mkx +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal, guide = "none") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0.5, n + 0.5, 1), labels = edge_lab, expand = c(0, 0)) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.5, 2.0), clip = "off") +
    labs(y = title) + theme_void(base_family = "Helvetica") +
    theme(axis.title.y = element_text(angle = 0, size = 7, hjust = 1, vjust = .5, margin = margin(r = 4)),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 5.5), axis.ticks.x = element_line(colour = "grey55", linewidth = .3),
          axis.ticks.length.x = unit(2, "pt"), plot.margin = margin(3, 26, 2, 34))
}

btitle <- function(txt) ggplot() + theme_void(base_family = "Helvetica") +
  annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0, label = txt, hjust = 0, size = 2.9, fontface = "bold") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1), clip = "off") + theme(plot.margin = margin(3, 2, 0, 26))

gC <- mapblock(bias, function(d) d$Bconf,        BIAS_PAL, BIAS_LIN,    top = TRUE)
gB <- mapblock(stat, function(d) log10(d$Dconf), DIST_PAL, DIST_BREAKS, bottom = TRUE)

# first moment before second, the natural order. The design points keep their numbering from the x
# axis (1 at the short interval, on the left of every map panel; 2 at the long one, on the right), so
# a reader finds them where the number says rather than where the story would put them.
g5 <- btitle("A   grouped MLE distributions") /
      gA /
      btitle("B   distortion-induced bias") /
      gC /
      colorbar(BIAS_PAL, BIAS_LAB, "bias (log10 units)", BIAS_LIN) /
      btitle("C   information distortion") /
      gB /
      colorbar(DIST_PAL, DIST_LAB, "distortion", DIST_LIN, sol = c(0.87, 1.15), dsh = c(0.67, 1.5)) +
      plot_layout(heights = c(0.16, 2.3, 0.16, 2.1, 0.28, 0.16, 2.25, 0.28))
g5
ggsave("Figure_5.pdf", g5, width = 7.0, height = 8.2)
```

```{r caption-numbers}
# Every number the caption quotes, printed on each knit so none of them is ever read off the plot.
cat("\n=== Figure 5 caption numbers ===\n\nAT THE THREE REFERENCE POINTS\n")
ref <- ELL_POINTS %>% rowwise() %>% do({
  pt <- .
  d <- stat %>% filter(comp == "total", Num_ch == pt$nch, noise == pt$noise,
                       abs(interval_in_tau - pt$dlt) < 1e-9, param %in% c("off","Num_ch_mean"))
  b <- bias %>% filter(Num_ch == pt$nch, noise == pt$noise,
                       abs(interval_in_tau - pt$dlt) < 1e-9, param %in% c("off","Num_ch_mean"))
  tibble(point = pt$lab, what = pt$what, N_ch = pt$nch, noise = pt$noise, delta = pt$dlt,
         D_koff = d$Dconf[d$param == "off"],       D_Nch = d$Dconf[d$param == "Num_ch_mean"],
         B_koff = b$Bconf[b$param == "off"],       B_Nch = b$Bconf[b$param == "Num_ch_mean"])
}) %>% ungroup()
print(as.data.frame(ref), digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nDISTORTION along the interval, k_off, at the column that carries the failure (N_ch 10, noise 0.05)\n")
print(as.data.frame(stat %>% filter(comp == "total", param == "off", Num_ch == 10, noise == "0.05") %>%
        arrange(desc(interval_in_tau)) %>% select(delta = interval_in_tau, Dconf)),
      digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nBIAS along the same line (log10 units), both parameters\n")
print(as.data.frame(bias %>% filter(Num_ch == 10, noise == "0.05", param %in% c("off","Num_ch_mean")) %>%
        select(param, interval_in_tau, Bconf) %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = param, values_from = Bconf) %>% arrange(desc(interval_in_tau))),
      digits = 3, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nEXTENT: fraction of (interval, noise) cells that depart significantly, per parameter and N_ch\n")
cat("  distortion, |D-1| >= 0.15:\n")
print(as.data.frame(stat %>% filter(comp == "total", param %in% c("off","Num_ch_mean")) %>% group_by(param, Num_ch) %>%
        summarise(f = mean(abs(Dconf - 1) >= 0.15), .groups = "drop") %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = f)), digits = 2, row.names = FALSE)
cat("  bias, interval excludes 0:\n")
print(as.data.frame(bias %>% filter(param %in% c("off","Num_ch_mean")) %>% group_by(param, Num_ch) %>%
        summarise(f = mean(Bconf != 0), .groups = "drop") %>%
        pivot_wider(names_from = Num_ch, values_from = f)), digits = 2, row.names = FALSE)
```
